overview.short: Maa Mangala Mandir is a Hindu Shakta pitha located in Kakatpur of Odisha, on the eastern coast of India.

topPlaces.name: Kakatpur
topPlaces.description: Kakatpur is a semi-urban town in Puri district in the Indian state of Odisha.

topPlaces.name: Kadua River
topPlaces.description: Kadua river is located in Odisha state, India, and formed by the confluence of two streams at Charigan village. The 22-mile long Kadua drains the area between Prachi and Kushabadra rivers before joining the Bay of Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Astaranga
topPlaces.description: Astaranga is a fishing and agricultural village and a community development block in Puri district in the Indian state of Odisha. It was a part of 'Lavana Satyagraha' (Salt March), a nonviolent civil disobedience movement in colonial India led by Mahatma Gandhi.

topPlaces.name: Prachi River
topPlaces.description: The Prachi, a small river of over 78 km in length with a catchment area of around 600 km2, is a part of the Mahanadi River Delta in Odisha along the eastern coast of India. Presently the parts of the modern day districts of Puri, Khurda, Cuttack and Jagatsingpur comprise the Prachi valley region.

items.note: Kakatpur is a semi-urban town in Puri district in the Indian state of Odisha.

Kadua river is located in Odisha state, India, and formed by the confluence of two streams at Charigan village. The 22-mile long Kadua drains the area between Prachi and Kushabadra rivers before joining the Bay of Bengal.

Astaranga is a fishing and agricultural village and a community development block in Puri district in the Indian state of Odisha. It was a part of 'Lavana Satyagraha' (Salt March), a nonviolent civil disobedience movement in colonial India led by Mahatma Gandhi.

The Prachi, a small river of over 78 km in length with a catchment area of around 600 km2, is a part of the Mahanadi River Delta in Odisha along the eastern coast of India. Presently the parts of the modern day districts of Puri, Khurda, Cuttack and Jagatsingpur comprise the Prachi valley region.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kakatpur Mangala Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kakatpur Mangala Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kakatpur Mangala Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Biju Patnaik International Airport, Bhubaneswar (48 km) and the nearest railway station is Puri Railway Station (44 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kakatpur Mangala Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kakatpur Mangala Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kakatpur Mangala Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Kakatpur Mangala Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kakatpur Mangala Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kakatpur, Kadua River, Astaranga, Prachi River.
